description: L3 targeting qualification for Poka Labs. Identifies high-value chemical/technical companies based on SKU breadth, product complexity, and company maturity. Run ONLY on L2-qualified domains.

waterfall_filter.description: Quick check that site has visible product catalog before deeper analysis

questions.prompt: Does this website have a visible product catalog, product listings, or product pages showing multiple products they sell? Look for: Products menu, Shop section, Product categories, SKU listings.

disqualify_rules.log_message: No visible product catalog on website

waterfall_filter.note: UNKNOWN passes through to allow iteration - homepage may not show products but inner pages might

output_schema.catalog_evidence: brief description of what catalog/products were found

waterfall_filter.closing_instruction: Be lenient. Say YES if there are visible products/listings. Say UNKNOWN if you see navigation suggesting products exist (Products menu, Solutions link, etc.) but can't see actual listings on this page. Only say NO if this is clearly NOT a product-selling company (pure services, holding company, etc.).

categories.name: TIER_1_PRIME
categories.description: Ideal target: Large SKU count (100+), high complexity (variants/grades), clear maturity signals. Highest priority for outreach.

categories.name: TIER_2_STRONG
categories.description: Strong target: Medium-large SKU count (50+), moderate complexity, some maturity signals. Good outreach candidate.

categories.name: TIER_3_POTENTIAL
categories.description: Potential target: Smaller SKU count (20+), some complexity indicators, limited maturity signals. Worth considering.

categories.name: TIER_4_SMALL
categories.description: Small operation: Few products (<20), minimal complexity, unclear maturity. Lower priority.

categories.name: DISQUALIFIED_NO_CATALOG
categories.description: Cannot assess - no visible product catalog or listings on website

categories.name: INSUFFICIENT_INFO
categories.description: Website accessible but cannot determine SKU breadth or complexity from available pages

product_categories_found.description: List of distinct product categories or lines observed (3-10 examples)

complexity_indicators.description: List of complexity signals found: grades, variants, custom formulations, specs, etc.

maturity_signals.description: List of maturity indicators found: certifications, locations, years, team, etc.

questions.question: How many distinct products/SKUs does this company appear to sell? Count unique product names, not variants. Look at product pages, catalogs, product menus.
questions.guidance: Count product TYPES not variants. A company selling 'Adhesive A' in 5 sizes = 1 product. Look at: product category counts, catalog pages, product menu depth.

questions.question: How complex are their product offerings? Look for: multiple grades/variants per product, custom formulation services, detailed technical specifications, application-specific versions.
questions.guidance: HIGH = products come in multiple grades/specs, custom formulation offered, detailed TDS for each variant. MEDIUM = some variants exist, basic specs. LOW = single version of each product, minimal specs.

questions.question: What specific evidence of product variants/grades did you find? List examples like 'Product X comes in Grade A, B, C' or 'Available in 5 viscosity grades'.
Does the company offer custom formulation, toll manufacturing, or made-to-spec products?
What quality/industry certifications are mentioned? (ISO 9001, ISO 14001, FDA, NSF, Kosher, REACH, etc.)
What signals indicate company scale/maturity? Look for: multiple locations, manufacturing facilities, warehouse square footage, years in business, employee count, established date.
Based on all signals, how mature/established does this company appear?
questions.guidance: HIGHLY_MATURE = multiple certifications + scale signals + professional presence. MODERATELY_MATURE = some certifications OR scale signals. EARLY_STAGE = minimal indicators.

questions.question: Is the website professionally designed with detailed product information, or is it basic/minimal?
Does the site have e-commerce / online ordering capability, or is it inquiry/quote-based only?

classification_logic.description: Tier assignment based on SKU breadth, complexity, and maturity signals

rules.condition: (sku_breadth in [VERY_LARGE_500+, LARGE_100-500]) AND (sku_complexity in [HIGH_COMPLEXITY, MEDIUM_COMPLEXITY]) AND (maturity_score in [HIGHLY_MATURE, MODERATELY_MATURE])

tier_summary.TIER_1_PRIME: 100+ SKUs + complex products + mature company = ideal target
tier_summary.TIER_3_POTENTIAL: 20-100 SKUs OR high complexity = worth considering

sufficiency_check.prompt_addition: Focus on finding product catalog pages. If homepage doesn't show products, look for Products, Catalog, Shop, or Categories links. The goal is to estimate how many products they sell and how complex those products are.

page_priority.description: Prioritize pages that reveal product breadth

tier_1_prime.description: Chemical distributor with 2000+ products across 50 categories, ISO 9001/14001 certified, 5 warehouse locations, online ordering
tier_1_prime.signals: VERY_LARGE SKUs, HIGH complexity (multiple grades), HIGHLY_MATURE

tier_1_prime.description: Specialty adhesives manufacturer with 500+ formulations, custom blending services, 75 years in business, multiple facilities

tier_2_strong.description: Polymer supplier with 150 products in 8 categories, some custom compounding, ISO 9001
Lab chemical supplier with 200+ reagents, multiple grades per product, established 1990

tier_3_potential.description: Coating manufacturer with 40 products, custom color matching available, single location
Specialty chemical producer with 30 products but very complex specs and variants

tier_4_small.description: Small resin producer with 8 products, no variants, minimal company info
Startup additive company with 5 products, basic website
